title: ESG Ratings Firm Defends Northveil Downgrade as Methane Data Row Intensifies
body:
Sustain Ratings on Wednesday stood by its decision to downgrade Northveil Energy Partners' environmental score, saying the satellite methane data it relied on was "peer-reviewed and consistent with three independent measurement campaigns."

Northveil CFO Warren Gault has disputed the methodology since Friday, arguing that satellite estimates have known limitations in complex terrain and that the company's own continuous monitoring equipment — independently calibrated in 2025 — contradicts the findings.

The dispute has spilled into regulatory territory. A spokesperson for FTC Commissioner Angela Torres noted this week that the commission has been "monitoring the reliability of ESG ratings providers" as part of a broader inquiry into disclosure standards in financial markets. The spokesperson declined to say whether Northveil or Sustain Ratings was specifically under review.

The failed bond offering has drawn attention from green finance advocates who argue that unstable ESG ratings infrastructure is hampering the clean energy transition. Renata Solis, a Washington lobbyist whose clients include several renewable energy developers, said in a statement that "the chaotic state of ESG credentialing is creating real harm for projects that genuinely deserve capital."

Northveil said it is in discussions with its underwriting banks about relaunching the bond offering once the ratings dispute is resolved, potentially as early as April.
